body:
## Next Steps to Merge
Only failed checks and required actions are listed below.

- ❌ **js - pullrequest (Build UnitTest windows_20x_browser)**: Browser unit test failure in the ADO pipeline. Action: Check the [ADO build results](https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/29ec6040-b234-4e31-b139-33dc4287b756/_build/results?buildId=6092191&view=results) for details. If tests have stale recordings, update them per the [test recording guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md#run-tests-in-record-mode).

<!-- mgmt-review-assistant -->

🔴 **Design Concern** — `CHANGELOG.md:3`

`## 19.0.0 (2026-03-31)`

`18.0.0` was released on `2025-11-18`, which is approximately 4.5 months before this `19.0.0` release — within the 6-month threshold. Frequent major version bumps (breaking changes) are not recommended as they impose migration burden on customers.

**Fix:** Please confirm the necessity of this major version bump at this time. If this is a TypeSpec migration that inherently requires breaking changes, add a note explaining the migration context (e.g., "This is a TypeSpec-generated SDK migration from the Swagger-based SDK") so customers can understand the rationale.

1 design concern found. All other API surface checks passed — no `_N` suffixes, no `AutoGenerated` suffixes, no `_` prefixes, no problematic `unknown`/`void` return types, no forgotten exports, and version consistency is confirmed across `package.json` (`19.0.0`), `src/api/webSiteManagementContext.ts` (`azsdk-js-arm-appservice/19.0.0`), and `CHANGELOG.md` (`19.0.0`). This is a TypeSpec migration from the Swagger-based `18.0.0` SDK.

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37912,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"CHANGELOG.md","line":3,"issueType":"design","category":"breaking_change_frequency","description":"19.0.0 released 4.5 months after 18.0.0 (2025-11-18), within the 6-month threshold for frequent major version bumps."}]}
```

</details>
